compiled_state.title: As a DSO user, I can run ticket list at scale without timeout
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Description

**What**: Replace the per-ticket subprocess pattern in the ticket list shell command with a batch reducer that compiles all tickets in a single Python process invocation.
**Why**: ticket list currently takes ~17 seconds for 445 tickets (~300ms per ticket due to Python subprocess startup). This causes intermittent failures in sprint workflows.
**Scope**:
- IN: New batch reduce mode in ticket-reducer.py, integration into ticket list command, benchmark script with threshold-based exit codes
- OUT: Changes to ticket show, ticket transition, or other per-ticket commands (they continue using the existing per-ticket reducer)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, ticket list completes in under 3 seconds at 300 open tickets and under 10 seconds at 1,000 open tickets
  <- Satisfies: 'ticket list completes in under 3 seconds at 300 open tickets and under 10 seconds at 1,000 open tickets'
- When this story is complete, a benchmark script seeds N ticket directories, times ticket list, and exits non-zero when thresholds are breached
  <- Satisfies: 'verified by a benchmark script that seeds N ticket directories, times the command, and exits non-zero when thresholds are breached'
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] Core ticket infrastructure change — ensure regression tests cover the existing ticket list output format
- [Maintainability] Story 3 also modifies ticket-reducer.py — coordinate to avoid merge conflicts
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Story complete: batch reducer implemented, ticket-list.sh optimized, benchmark script created. All tests GREEN.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
